trajectory.description: Observation: Starting with empty workspace on fMoW WILDS task (62-class satellite classification, worst-region acc metric). Analysis: Must establish baseline quickly per instructions - use CLIP ViT-B/16 vision encoder + linear head (fits <1B param limit, leverages pretrained visual features for satellite imagery). Train 3 epochs initially with DataParallel on 4 GPUs to measure time/validate pipeline. Decision: Write all core files (model, train, predict, dataset utils), run short training, then submit_eval for first score and timing measurement.

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP ViT-B/16 + linear head (3 epochs full FT) gave worst-region acc 0.0019 / overall 0.022 (barely above random), first-epoch NaN, val acc ~2%. Analysis: full backbone FT with LR=1e-4 ineffective for quick learning on this shift-heavy task; head undertrained, NaNs suggest instability. Decision: freeze vision backbone (linear probe on strong pretrained features), train classifier only with higher LR=1e-3 + 15 epochs, add H/V flips aug (suitable for overhead sat imagery), use val worst-region acc (computed over 5 regions, ignore -1) for best checkpoint selection, add grad clipping. This should yield big gains in worst-region acc while staying under time/iter budget (prior iter ~17min for 3eps → ~1.4h for 15eps). Keep predict.py/model.py unchanged.

Observation: Iter1 full FT gave worst-region 0.0019/overall 0.022; iter2 frozen linear probe exploded to loss~96 with ~1.5% val acc (below random). Analysis: CLIPVisionModel.pooler_output has poor scale for classifier (no visual_projection); freezing prevents domain adaptation to satellite; high LR on mismatched features causes instability. Decision: Refactor model to CLIPModel.get_image_features() for proper 512-dim embeddings, use differential LR (1e-5 vision+proj, 1e-3 head), label smoothing=0.1, 10 epochs, keep aug+clip_grad. This stabilizes training and leverages CLIP better. Submit eval after to get baseline score+time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed immediately with TypeError (linear input was BaseModelOutputWithPooling instead of Tensor); prior runs showed near-random worst-region acc (~0.002). Workspace train.py was truncated/incomplete at save logic. Analysis: CLIPModel.get_image_features can return structured output depending on return_dict/config/version; explicit vision_model(pooler_output) + visual_projection ensures Tensor. Fixed by refactoring model forward, completing train.py (full save, WARNING logging only, batch=128 for stability, drop_last, robust val calc), 5 epochs for timing. Decision: Run fixed training to establish usable baseline, submit_eval on best.pt immediately after to measure progress on primary metric (expect large gain from proper CLIP features + diff-LR head).

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter4 with fixed CLIP-B/16 (diff LR 1e-5/1e-3, label smoothing, 5eps) reached worst_region_accuracy=0.3652 (Africa worst, overall 0.512). Loss plateaued ~3.56 (above random~4.1 but not decreasing much), val worst~0.138 during training. Prior iters near-random (<0.002). Train.py was truncated at save but completed successfully; predict/model stable. Analysis: Proper CLIP image_features + head tuning gives strong baseline lift via pretrained vision priors on sat imagery; limited backbone adaptation and small model limit further gains on geographic/temporal shift (Africa hardest). Larger ViT-L/14 (still <1B params), more epochs (8), enhanced augs (90deg rotations for overhead), vision LR=5e-5, robust save+logging will improve. Decision: Update model.py default to large-patch14, fully rewrite train.py (fix save, add rotation aug+color jitter, proper DataParallel handling, metrics logging, best-save by val worst-acc), keep predict/dataset, run 8 epochs then submit_eval for next score/timing.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter5 with CLIP ViT-L/14 + linear head, diff LR (5e-5/1e-3), augs (flips/rot/color), 8 epochs reached worst_region=0.3941 (Africa worst, overall 0.60); val worst peaked at 0.168 during training with loss plateauing ~3.74 (slow descent from ~3.76, random~4.13). Prior random-level runs fixed by proper image_features+proj. Analysis: Strong baseline from CLIP priors on sat imagery but limited by linear head on imbalanced 62-class + geographic/temporal shift (Africa hardest); conservative vision LR and weak aug limit adaptation. Train.py truncated but functional (saves best by val worst-acc, uses DataParallel). Decision: Upgrade to 2-layer MLP head w/ dropout+ReLU for capacity, add RandAugment for stronger augs, raise vision LR to 1e-4 for faster adaptation (w/ grad clip), set 12 epochs (~4h fit in budget), robust state_dict loading in predict. Rewrite train.py fully (complete logic, structured logging, best-save by worst-acc), update model+predict, run training then submit_eval for improved score/timing.
